_description: Core Guards - Security and SOLID enforcement hooks (enforcement + lifecycle delegated to @fusengine/harness)

PreToolUse._description: Harness: guards (security/bash-write/git/install/interface-separation/protected-path), file-size, APEX gates (freshness/doc/solid/brainstorm), MCP+WebFetch cache & verbosity cap. Matcher restricted to the enforced tools — Read/Glob/Grep are NOT routed here (the APEX gate keys off filePath presence and would wrongly deny plain reads).

PostToolUse._description: Harness: record agent/doc/ref-read activity + MCP cache store + file-size warn, PLUS (Write/Edit) cumulative session-change tracking → sniper reminder + eslint/prettier report. One harness call (ports track-session-changes.py + post-edit-typescript.py).

SessionStart._description: Harness: inject ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md + dev context (git/project type) and run cache/state cleanups (ports the four session-start/*.py into one call)

UserPromptSubmit._description: Harness: read ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md (+ APEX preamble on dev verbs) injected as additionalContext AND detect creation intent → set brainstorm-required flag. One harness call does both.

SubagentStart._description: Harness: inject fresh MCP cache (Context7/Exa) entries into the sub-agent prompt (ports inject-context-cache.py)

SubagentStop._description: Harness: track agent completion + memory and emit sniper reminder on code changes (ports track-agent-memory.py)

TeammateIdle._description: Harness: suggest sniper validation when a teammate modified code before going idle (ports validate-teammate-output.py)

TaskCompleted._description: Harness: re-measure the task's modified code files and flag SOLID violations over the line ceiling (ports task-completed/validate-task-solid.py)

PostToolUseFailure._description: Harness: log tool failures to ~/.claude/logs/tool-failures.log (ports log-tool-failure.py)

Stop._description: Sound notification when Claude finishes responding (not on user interrupt)
Verify APEX workflow completion before stopping

hooks._description: Verify APEX workflow completion before stopping
hooks.prompt: Check if the assistant followed the APEX workflow. Look for: 1) Was an explore/research agent launched before coding? 2) Were files kept under 100 lines? 3) Was sniper validation run after modifications? If any step was missed AND code was modified, respond with {"ok": false, "reason": "Missing step: [describe what was missed]"}. If no code was modified or all steps were followed, respond with {"ok": true}. Context: $ARGUMENTS

PermissionRequest._description: Sound for ALL permission dialogs (system + hook-triggered)

PreCompact._description: Harness: back up .claude/apex/task.json before compaction, keep 5 newest (ports save-apex-state.py)

SessionEnd._description: Harness: remove stale temp/legacy cache files on session end (ports cleanup-session.py)

InstructionsLoaded._description: Harness: log loaded instruction files for APEX debugging (ports validate-rules-loaded.py)

Notification._description: Sound for permission requests (Bash, Write, Edit confirmations)
Sound when Claude waits for user input (60+ sec idle)
Sound for MCP tool elicitation dialogs
